perturbation.original_text: 10. Any dispute, controv ersy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or any related agreement or other document or the validity, interpretation, breach or termination thereof ( Dispute'), including claims seeking redress or asserting rights under applicable law, shal l, be resolved amicably between the parties and in case the dispute could not be settled by mutual negotiation, then the same shall be referred to a sole arbitrator appointed by the Bank and the decision of the Arbitrator shall be final and bi nding on the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Any dispute, controv ersy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or any related agreement or other document or the validity, interpretation, breach or termination thereof ( Dispute'), including claims seeking redress or asserting rights under applicable law, shal l, be resolved amicably between the parties and in case the dispute could not be settled by mutual negotiation, then the same shall be referred to a arbitrator appointed by both party and the decision of the Arbitrator shall be reviewed in court.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the arbitration clause, we create an in-text contradiction. The original states that the arbitrator's decision is 'final and binding,' implying no further appeal. The modified version states 'the decision of the Arbitrator shall be reviewed in court,' suggesting that the arbitrator's decision is not final and can be challenged in court. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the finality of arbitration decisions, which can lead to one of the party going to court after the decision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Any dispute, controv ersy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or any related agreement or other document or the validity, interpretation, breach or termination thereof ( Dispute'), including claims seeking redress or asserting rights under applicable law, shal l, be resolved amicably between the parties and in case the dispute could not be settled by mutual negotiation, then the same shall be referred to a arbitrator appointed by both party and the decision of the Arbitrator shall be reviewed in court.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the arbitrator's decision is 'final and binding,' implying no further appeal. The changed text states 'the decision of the Arbitrator shall be reviewed in court,' suggesting the opposite. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, the change introduces a contradiction by directly contradicting the finality of the arbitration decision within the same clause. This creates uncertainty about whether the arbitrator's decision is truly final and creates a strong contradiction in what was once consistent.

perturbation.original_text: 13. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the matters discussed herein and supersedes any and all prior oral discussions and/o r writte n Correspondence or agreements between the parties. This Agreement may be amended or modified only with the mutual written consent of the parties . Neith er this Agreement nor any right granted hereunder shall be assignable or otherwise transferable .
perturbation.changed_text: 13. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the matters discussed herein and supersedes any and all prior oral discussions and/o r writte n Correspondence or agreements between the parties. This Agreement may be amended or modified verbally . Neith er this Agreement nor any right granted hereunder shall be assignable or otherwise transferable .
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the agreement can only be modified with 'mutual written consent'. The modified version states that the agreement can be amended 'verbally'. This is an explicit contradiction. It's unclear whether a verbal agreement is valid or not, which creates a potential dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the matters discussed herein and supersedes any and all prior oral discussions and/o r writte n Correspondence or agreements between the parties. This Agreement may be amended or modified verbally . Neith er this Agreement nor any right granted hereunder shall be assignable or otherwise transferable .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement could only be modified with mutual written consent. The changed text explicitly states that the agreement can be modified verbally. This creates a direct and undeniable contradiction within the same section, making it unclear whether a verbal agreement is valid and enforceable. The changed text and contradicted text are the same, but that doesn't change the fact that the modification created an in-text contradiction, as per the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Receiving Party shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless to the Disclosing Party, its affiliates, subsidiaries, successors, assigns, officers, directors, employees, at all times, from and against any and a ll claims, demands, damages, assertions of liability whether civil, criminal, tortuous or of an nature whatsoever, arising out of or pertaining to or resulting from any breach of representations and warranties made by Receiving Party and/or breach o f any provisions of this Agreement, but not limited to any claim from third party pursuant to any act or omission of Receiving Party in the course of discharge of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Receiving Party shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless to the Disclosing Party, its affiliates, subsidiaries, successors, assigns, officers, directors, employees, only in case of fraud, from and against any and a ll claims, demands, damages, assertions of liability whether civil, criminal, tortuous or of an nature whatsoever, arising out of or pertaining to or resulting from any breach of representations and warranties made by Receiving Party and/or breach o f any provisions of this Agreement, but not limited to any claim from third party pursuant to any act or omission of Receiving Party in the course of discharge of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the Receiving Party will fully indemnify the Disclosing Party at all times. The modified clause states the Receiving Party will indemnify only in case of fraud. This in-text contradiction severely limits the Receiving Party's indemnification obligations and now requires the other party to proof that the breach was done via fraud. So there is no clarity if the Receiving Party will fully indemnify the Disclosing Party
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Receiving Party shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless to the Disclosing Party, its affiliates, subsidiaries, successors, assigns, officers, directors, employees, only in case of fraud, from and against any and a ll claims, demands, damages, assertions of liability whether civil, criminal, tortuous or of an nature whatsoever, arising out of or pertaining to or resulting from any breach of representations and warranties made by Receiving Party and/or breach o f any provisions of this Agreement, but not limited to any claim from third party pursuant to any act or omission of Receiving Party in the course of discharge of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party will fully indemnify the Disclosing Party at all times. The changed text modifies this to state the Receiving Party will indemnify only in case of fraud. This directly contradicts the original broad indemnification obligation, creating a conflict with the contradicted text, which contains the changed clause itself. This clearly limits the Receiving Party's indemnification obligations, creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Neither party shall make news releases, public announcement s, give interviews, issue or publish advertisements or publicize in any other manner whatsoever in connectio n with this Agreement , the content s / provision s thereof , other information relating to this Agreement, the Purpose, the Confidential Information or other matter of this Agreement, without the prior written approval of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Either party is encouraged to make news releases, public announcement s, give interviews, issue or publish advertisements or publicize in any other manner whatsoever in connectio n with this Agreement , the content s / provision s thereof , other information relating to this Agreement, the Purpose, the Confidential Information or other matter of this Agreement, with the prior written approval of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Neither party shall make' to 'Either party is encouraged to make', the modification introduces a contradiction with the spirit and purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement. NDAs are intended to prevent the dissemination of confidential information, while this change encourages it, which creates uncertainty regarding the confidentiality obligations and could damage the relationship between parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. “Confidential Information” means and includes all data, documents and information disclosed or to be disclosed by either Party to other Party and its Representatives (as defined herein) (including i nformation transmitted in written, electronic, magnetic or other form and also information transmitted orally or visually) and any and all information which may be developed or created, in whole or in part, directly or indirectly, from such information including all notes, summaries, an alyses, compilations and other writings.  “Receiving Party” means party who receives Confidential Information.  “Disclosing Party” means party who discloses Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibited either party from making public announcements without prior written approval. The changed text encourages either party to make such announcements with prior written approval. This directly contradicts the purpose of maintaining confidentiality as defined in the contradicted text, which broadly defines 'Confidential Information' and aims to protect it. Encouraging public announcements clashes with the intention to keep information confidential, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. All Confidential Information is provided “as it is”. In no event shall Disclosing Party be liable for the inaccuracy or incompleteness of the Confid ential Information. None of the Confidential Information disclosed by the parties constitutes any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by either party to the other with respect to the fitness of such Confidential Information for any particular purpose or infringement of trademarks, patents, copyrights or any right of third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. All Confidential Information is provided with a warranty of accuracy. In the event that any inaccuracy or incompleteness of the Confidential Information causes financial damages, the Disclosing Party will be held liable. The Confidential Information disclosed by the parties constitutes a guarantee by the disclosing party to the other with respect to the fitness of such Confidential Information for any particular purpose .
perturbation.explanation: The original clause disclaims any liability for the inaccuracy of the information. The changed clause makes the Disclosing Party liable for the inaccuracy of the information. The original stated there are no guarantees of fitness, but now there are. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the liability and warranties associated with the confidential information. This directly contradicts 6 because the Receiving Party, now can make the Disclosing Party liable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. All Confidential Information is provided with a warranty of accuracy. In the event that any inaccuracy or incompleteness of the Confidential Information causes financial damages, the Disclosing Party will be held liable. The Confidential Information disclosed by the parties constitutes a guarantee by the disclosing party to the other with respect to the fitness of such Confidential Information for any particular purpose .
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text regarding liability for inaccuracy and guarantees of fitness, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly disclaims liability and warranties, while the changed text imposes liability and provides a guarantee. The Changed Text imposes a warranty of accuracy on Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original clause which states that Confidential Information is provided "as is" and explicitly disclaims any guarantees. This directly breaks compatibility and creates a strong inconsistency, making the answer YES.
